name: Depression - Antidepressant Medication Query
description: Tests ability to find depression patients via antidepressant prescriptions. The PheKB algorithm uses antidepressant medications as one of four longitudinal data sources. Synthea distributes patients across multiple drug classes — SSRIs (sertraline, fluoxetine, escitalopram, citalopram), SNRI (venlafaxine), and atypical (bupropion). An LLM that only queries one drug or one class will substantially underestimate the depression population.
prompt: Retrieve medication orders for patients prescribed antidepressants such as SSRIs (selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors), SNRIs (serotonin-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitors), or atypical antidepressants.

prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed antidepressant medications. The most common classes are SSRIs (sertraline/Zoloft, fluoxetine/Prozac, escitalopram/Lexapro, citalopram/Celexa, paroxetine/Paxil), SNRIs (venlafaxine/Effexor, duloxetine/Cymbalta), and atypicals like bupropion (Wellbutrin).
prompts.expert: I need to find all patients on antidepressant therapy. Synthea generates these at the SCD level — search MedicationRequest for these RxNorm codes: 312938 (Sertraline 100 MG), 310385 (Fluoxetine 20 MG cap), 351250 (Escitalopram 20 MG), 200371 (Citalopram 20 MG), 313580 (Venlafaxine 100 MG), and 993537 (Bupropion HCl 200 MG ER [Wellbutrin]). Note that querying ingredient codes will not match SCD-coded prescriptions in HAPI FHIR — you must query the specific SCD codes.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients on antidepressants (Paths A and C). Maps to PheKB depression algorithm's antidepressant medication criterion. Includes Path C tricky patients on SSRIs without a formal depression diagnosis.

required_codes.display: 12 HR bupropion hydrochloride 200 MG Extended Release Oral Tablet [Wellbutrin]
